notes_md:
**Coi** = to watch, look at, or check something. It’s used for watching TV, movies, sports, or looking at something.

As a verb, it goes straight before the object: **coi + something** (coi phim, coi tivi, coi bản đồ). It can also mean “check” or “take a look at” in casual speech.

Very common synonym: **xem**. In the North, people usually say *xem*; in the South, *coi* is used a lot in everyday speech.

It can also mean “consider/regard” in phrases like *coi như* (meaning “consider as” or “treat as”).

translation.en: I want to watch this movie.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Do you want to watch a movie with me tonight?
I usually watch TV after class to relax.
Every weekend, my whole family watches football together.

example.en: Watch out for vehicles when crossing the street.
example.es: Ten cuidado con los vehículos al cruzar la calle.
example.vi: Coi chừng xe cộ khi băng qua đường.

definitions.en: To look at or observe something carefully.
definitions.es: Mirar u observar algo con atención.
definitions.vi: Nhìn hoặc quan sát một cái gì đó một cách cẩn thận.

content.sentence: Chúng ta có thể ___ chương trình này không?

notes:
Coi = to watch, look at, or check something. It’s used for watching TV, movies, sports, or looking at something.

As a verb, it goes straight before the object: coi + something (coi phim, coi tivi, coi bản đồ). It can also mean “check” or “take a look at” in casual speech.

Very common synonym: xem. In the North, people usually say xem; in the South, coi is used a lot in everyday speech.

It can also mean “consider/regard” in phrases like coi như (meaning “consider as” or “treat as”).
exampleSentences: Tối nay anh muốn coi phim với em không?
Em thường coi tivi sau giờ học để thư giãn.
Mỗi cuối tuần, cả nhà chị đều cùng nhau coi bóng đá.
